test_cases.test_case: Log in with valid credentials
preconditions: User is on the login page
User has valid username and password
steps: 1. Enter valid <username> in the Username field
2. Enter valid <password> in the Password field
3. Click 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to log in with invalid credentials
preconditions: User is on the login page
steps: 1. Enter invalid <username> in the Username field
2. Enter invalid <password> in the Password field
3. Click 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message is displayed; the password field is cleared and the username remains for correction

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to log in with empty fields
preconditions: User is on the login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field empty
2. Leave the Password field empty
3. Click 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message is displayed for both fields; both fields remain empty

test_cases.test_case: Log in with a very long username and password
preconditions: User is on the login page
steps: 1. Enter a username with maximum allowed length in the Username field
2. Enter a password with maximum allowed length in the Password field
3. Click 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Dashboard if credentials are valid; otherwise, an error message is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Log in with a username containing special characters
preconditions: User is on the login page
steps: 1. Enter a username with special characters (e.g., !@#$%^&*) in the Username field
2. Enter a valid <password> in the Password field
3. Click 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Dashboard if credentials are valid; otherwise, an error message is displayed

test_cases.test_case: View the Dashboard with all elements displayed correctly
preconditions: User has enrolled in at least one course
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard
2. Observe the personalized greeting at the top
3. Check that the Timeline block is displayed with the upcoming teaching actions
4. Verify that the Calendar block is displayed with the current month and year
test_cases.expected_result: The Dashboard displays a personalized greeting, the Timeline block shows upcoming actions, and the Calendar block shows the current month and year

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to use the search field in the Timeline block without entering a search term
preconditions: User has enrolled in at least one course
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard
2. Locate the search field in the Timeline block
3. Leave the search field empty
4. Click the search button
test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating that a search term must be entered; no search results are shown

test_cases.test_case: Select a time range in the Timeline block that has no upcoming actions
preconditions: User has enrolled in at least one course
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard
2. Locate the time range dropdown in the Timeline block
3. Select 'Next 7 days' (assuming no actions are scheduled in this range)
4. Observe the Timeline block
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block displays an empty state message indicating no upcoming actions are available for the selected time range

test_cases.test_case: Create a new event in the Calendar block
preconditions: User has access to the Calendar block
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard
2. Click the 'New event' button in the Calendar block
3. Fill in the event details (title, date, time)
4. Click 'Save'
test_cases.expected_result: The new event appears in the Calendar block on the selected date

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to create a new event without filling in required fields
preconditions: User has access to the Calendar block
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard
2. Click the 'New event' button in the Calendar block
3. Leave all required fields empty
4. Click 'Save'
test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating that required fields must be filled; the event is not created

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to the previous month in the Calendar block
preconditions: User has access to the Calendar block
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard
2. Locate the Calendar block
3. Click the left arrow to navigate to the previous month
test_cases.expected_result: The Calendar block updates to display the previous month with the correct month and year heading

test_cases.test_case: Add a block to the Dashboard in Edit mode
steps: 1. Click '+ Add a block' below the Dashboard heading
2. Select 'Course overview' from the list of available block types
3. Click 'Add' to add the block to the Dashboard
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Course overview' block appears on the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to add a block when Edit mode is off
steps: 1. Click '+ Add a block' below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: The '+ Add a block' button is not clickable; no action is performed

test_cases.test_case: Reset the Dashboard to default after adding multiple blocks
preconditions: Multiple blocks are added to the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click 'Reset page to default' at the top right
2. Confirm the reset action
test_cases.expected_result: All blocks are removed and the Dashboard returns to its default state

test_cases.test_case: Open the options menu for a block
preconditions: At least one block is present on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click the three-dot options menu on an existing block
2. Select 'Configure' from the menu
test_cases.expected_result: The configuration settings for the selected block are displayed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to reset the Dashboard without any blocks added
preconditions: No blocks are added to the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click 'Reset page to default' at the top right
2. Confirm the reset action
test_cases.expected_result: A message indicates that there are no changes to revert; the Dashboard remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: View all courses displayed as visual cards
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
test_cases.expected_result: All courses the teacher has access to are displayed as visual cards with course banner images, names, and category names

test_cases.test_case: Search for a specific course by name
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Enter <course name> in the search field
3. Click the search button
test_cases.expected_result: Only the course matching <course name> is displayed in the course cards

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to search for a course with an empty search field
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Leave the search field empty
3. Click the search button
test_cases.expected_result: All courses are displayed as visual cards; no error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Sort courses with maximum number of courses displayed
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Select the sort dropdown and choose <sorting criteria>
3. Observe the displayed courses
test_cases.expected_result: Courses are sorted according to the selected criteria, and all courses are displayed correctly

test_cases.test_case: Star a course to pin it to the top
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Click the three-dot menu on a course card
3. Select 'Star this course'
test_cases.expected_result: The starred course is pinned to the top of the course list

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to star a course that is already starred
preconditions: At least one course is already starred
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Click the three-dot menu on a starred course card
3. Select 'Star this course'
test_cases.expected_result: A message indicates that the course is already starred; no changes are made

test_cases.test_case: Remove a course from view and check the remaining courses
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Click the three-dot menu on a course card
3. Select 'Remove from view'
4. Refresh the My Courses page
test_cases.expected_result: The removed course no longer appears in the course list; other courses remain visible

test_cases.test_case: View course content with all sections expanded
preconditions: A course exists with multiple sections
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Ensure all sections are expanded
test_cases.expected_result: All sections are visible with activities and resources listed under each section

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to view course content without being logged in
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page with an appropriate error message

test_cases.test_case: Collapse all sections when no sections are expanded
preconditions: A course exists with all sections collapsed
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Click on the 'Collapse all' link
test_cases.expected_result: No change occurs; all sections remain collapsed and no error is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Collapse a specific section of the course
preconditions: A course exists with multiple sections
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Expand a specific section
3. Click on the collapsible chevron of that section
test_cases.expected_result: The specific section collapses, hiding its activities and resources

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to collapse a section that does not exist
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Click on a non-existent section's collapsible chevron
test_cases.expected_result: No action is performed; a message indicates the section does not exist

modules.module: Course Edit Mode and Activity Chooser

test_cases.test_case: Enable Edit mode and access Activity Chooser
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Click the 'Turn editing on' button
3. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' in a section
test_cases.expected_result: The Activity Chooser modal opens, displaying categories and activity/resource tiles

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to add an activity without enabling Edit mode
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' in a section
test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears indicating that Edit mode must be enabled to add activities

test_cases.test_case: Add multiple activities using bulk actions
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' in a section
3. Select multiple activities from the Activity Chooser
4. Click 'Add' to add all selected activities
test_cases.expected_result: All selected activities are added to the course section successfully

test_cases.test_case: Create an assignment with all required fields filled
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' in the target section
3. Select Assignment from the Activity Chooser and click Add
4. Enter <assignment name> in the Assignment name field
5. Enter <description> in the Description field
6. Enable the Due date toggle and select <due date>
7. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: The assignment appears in the course section with a link; the Grading summary panel shows 0 submissions

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save an assignment without filling the required fields
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page and click '+ Add an activity or resource'
2. Select Assignment and click Add
3. Leave the Assignment name field empty
4. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: An inline validation error highlights the Assignment name field; the form is not submitted and remains open

test_cases.test_case: Set both Due date and Cut-off date to the same time in the past
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Assignment creation form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Enable Due date toggle and set it to <specific past date and time>
4. Enable Cut-off date toggle and set it to the exact same past date and time as the due date
5. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment is saved successfully and appears in the course; both due and cut-off dates are identical in the assignment metadata

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save an assignment with invalid file types in File submissions
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page and click '+ Add an activity or resource'
2. Select Assignment and click Add
3. Enter <assignment name>
4. Enable File submissions and set invalid file types
5. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: An error message indicates that the file types are not accepted; the form is not submitted and remains open

test_cases.test_case: Set maximum submission size to the system's maximum limit
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Assignment creation form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Enable File submissions and set maximum submission size to <system maximum size>
4. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment is saved successfully and appears in the course; maximum submission size is set correctly in the assignment metadata

test_cases.test_case: Save course settings with all required fields filled correctly
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Settings page
2. Enter <course full name> in the Course full name field
3. Enter <course short name> in the Course short name field
4. Select <course category> from the Course category dropdown
5. Set Course visibility to <Show/Hide>
6. Optionally set Course start date and Course end date
7. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: The course settings are saved successfully and the user is redirected to the course page with updated settings

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save course settings without filling required fields
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Settings page
2. Leave the Course full name field empty
3. Leave the Course short name field empty
4. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors highlight the Course full name and Course short name fields; the form is not submitted and remains open

test_cases.test_case: Set maximum upload size to the system's maximum limit
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Settings page
2. Enter <course full name> in the Course full name field
3. Enter <course short name> in the Course short name field
4. Select <course category> from the Course category dropdown
5. Set Maximum upload size to the maximum allowed value
6. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: The course settings are saved successfully with the maximum upload size set; the user is redirected to the course page

test_cases.test_case: Enroll a user in the course successfully
preconditions: A course exists with users enrolled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Participants page of the course
2. Click the 'Enrol users' button
3. Enter a valid username in the user search field
4. Select a Role from the Role dropdown
5. Optionally set an Enrollment duration
6. Click 'Enrol selected users'
test_cases.expected_result: The user is added to the course with the specified role; the participants list updates to include the new user

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to enroll a user without selecting a role
preconditions: A course exists with users enrolled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Participants page of the course
2. Click the 'Enrol users' button
3. Enter a valid username in the user search field
4. Leave the Role dropdown unselected
5. Click 'Enrol selected users'
test_cases.expected_result: An error message indicates that a role must be selected; the user is not enrolled

test_cases.test_case: Filter participants using multiple conditions
preconditions: A course exists with multiple enrolled users
steps: 1. Navigate to the Participants page of the course
2. Click the '+ Add condition' link
3. Select 'First name' from the Select attribute dropdown
4. Set the condition to 'Contains' and enter a common substring
5. Click 'Apply filters'
test_cases.expected_result: The participants list updates to show only users whose first names contain the specified substring; the filter conditions are displayed

test_cases.test_case: Use alphabetical filter for Last name with no results
preconditions: A course exists with users enrolled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Participants page of the course
2. Click the 'Last name' alphabetical filter button for 'Z'
3. Observe the participants list
test_cases.expected_result: The participants list shows no users; a message indicates that no users match the filter criteria

test_cases.test_case: View assignment metadata including dates and description
preconditions: An assignment exists in the course
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Click on the assignment link to open the Assignment page
test_cases.expected_result: The assignment's metadata including Opened date, Due date, full Description, and any attached files is displayed correctly

test_cases.test_case: Access grading interface for an assignment
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Click on the assignment link to open the Assignment page
3. Click the 'Grade' button
test_cases.expected_result: The grading interface opens, displaying a list of students and their submissions

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to view assignment metadata when no assignment exists
preconditions: No assignments exist in the course
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Attempt to click on a non-existent assignment link
test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating that the assignment does not exist

test_cases.test_case: View assignment with maximum length description
preconditions: An assignment exists with a description at maximum character limit
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Click on the assignment link to open the Assignment page
test_cases.expected_result: The assignment's metadata is displayed correctly, and the full description is visible without truncation

test_cases.test_case: View all student submissions with default filters
preconditions: An assignment with student submissions exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment Submissions view
2. Observe the table displaying all student submission records
test_cases.expected_result: The table displays all student submissions with correct details in each column

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to filter submissions with invalid student name
preconditions: An assignment with student submissions exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment Submissions view
2. Enter an invalid student name in the search field
3. Click the 'Search' button
test_cases.expected_result: The table shows no results found; a message indicates that no submissions match the search criteria

test_cases.test_case: Filter submissions by grading status with no submissions
preconditions: An assignment exists with no submissions
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment Submissions view
2. Select 'Not graded' from the grading status filter
3. Click the 'Filter' button
test_cases.expected_result: The table remains empty, and a message indicates that there are no submissions to display

test_cases.test_case: Enable quick grading mode and enter grades
preconditions: An assignment with student submissions exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment Submissions view
2. Enable 'Quick grading' mode
3. Enter grades for multiple students directly in the table
4. Click 'Save changes'
test_cases.expected_result: Grades are saved successfully, and the table reflects the updated grades for each student

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to enter invalid grade in quick grading mode
preconditions: An assignment with student submissions exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment Submissions view
2. Enable 'Quick grading' mode
3. Enter an invalid grade (e.g., a negative number) for a student
4. Click 'Save changes'
test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears indicating that the grade is invalid; the grade is not saved

test_cases.test_case: Filter submissions by submission status with maximum entries
preconditions: An assignment exists with maximum allowed submissions
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment Submissions view
2. Select 'Submitted for grading' from the submission status filter
3. Click the 'Filter' button
test_cases.expected_result: The table displays all submissions that match the filter criteria, even if the number is at the system's maximum limit

test_cases.test_case: Switch from Grader report to User report
preconditions: A course with enrolled students exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Gradebook section
2. Locate the report-type selector dropdown at the top left
3. Select 'User report' from the dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: The page refreshes to display the User report with the list of students and their grades

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save a grade entry with a value outside the configured grade range
steps: 1. Navigate to the Gradebook section
2. Locate the grade cell for a specific student and activity
3. Enter a grade value that exceeds the maximum configured grade range
4. Click 'Save changes'
test_cases.expected_result: An inline validation error highlights the grade cell; the form is not submitted and remains open

test_cases.test_case: Edit a grade entry to the minimum boundary value
steps: 1. Navigate to the Gradebook section
2. Locate the grade cell for a specific student and activity
3. Change the grade entry to the minimum allowed value (e.g., 0)
4. Click 'Save changes'
test_cases.expected_result: The grade entry is saved successfully, and the updated value is displayed in the grade cell

test_cases.test_case: Filter students by name in the Gradebook
preconditions: A course with enrolled students exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Gradebook section
2. Locate the user search and filter controls above the grade table
3. Enter a student's name in the search field
4. Press Enter or click the search button
test_cases.expected_result: The grade table updates to display only the rows corresponding to the filtered student

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save changes without enabling Edit mode
steps: 1. Navigate to the Gradebook section
2. Locate the grade cell for a specific student and activity
3. Change the grade entry
4. Click 'Save changes'
test_cases.expected_result: An error message indicates that Edit mode must be enabled to save changes; no changes are saved

test_cases.test_case: Edit multiple grade entries in quick succession
steps: 1. Navigate to the Gradebook section
2. Quickly change several grade entries for different students
3. Click 'Save changes' after editing multiple entries
test_cases.expected_result: All changes are saved successfully, and the updated values are displayed in the grade cells

test_cases.test_case: View teacher's profile with all details displayed
steps: 1. Navigate to the Profile page
2. Observe the circular initials icon, full name, and 'Message' button
3. Check for the presence of the profile description (if provided)
4. Verify the information cards for User details, Privacy and policies, Course details, Miscellaneous, Reports, and Login activity
test_cases.expected_result: All profile elements are displayed correctly, including the initials icon, full name, and all information cards

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access profile without being logged in
steps: 1. Navigate to the Profile page
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page with an appropriate error message

test_cases.test_case: Profile page displays with a very long profile description
preconditions: Profile description is set to a very long text
steps: 1. Navigate to the Profile page
2. Observe the profile description section
test_cases.expected_result: The profile description is displayed correctly without breaking the layout; scrolling may be required to view the entire text

test_cases.test_case: Update profile with all required fields filled correctly
preconditions: User is on the Edit profile page
steps: 1. Enter 'John' in the First name field
2. Enter 'Doe' in the Last name field
3. Enter 'john.doe@example.com' in the Email address field
4. Select 'Show my email address' from the Email visibility dropdown
5. Enter '12345' in the MoodleNet profile ID field
6. Enter 'New York' in the City/town field
7. Select 'United States' from the Country dropdown
8. Select 'America/New_York' from the Timezone dropdown
9. Enter a description in the rich text editor
10. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: The profile is updated successfully; the user sees a confirmation message and the updated profile information.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to update profile without filling required fields
preconditions: User is on the Edit profile page
steps: 1. Leave the First name field empty
2. Leave the Last name field empty
3. Leave the Email address field empty
4. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors are displayed for First name, Last name, and Email address fields; the form is not submitted.

test_cases.test_case: Upload a user picture with maximum file size limit
preconditions: User is on the Edit profile page
steps: 1. Prepare a user picture file that is at the maximum allowed size
2. Drag and drop the picture file into the upload area
3. Enter a description in the Picture description field
4. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: The profile is updated successfully; the new picture is displayed with the provided description.

test_cases.test_case: Enter a very long description in the rich text editor
preconditions: User is on the Edit profile page
steps: 1. Enter 'John' in the First name field
2. Enter 'Doe' in the Last name field
3. Enter 'john.doe@example.com' in the Email address field
4. Enter a very long text (exceeding typical limits) in the Description field
5. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: The profile is updated successfully; the long description is saved and displayed correctly.

test_cases.test_case: Successfully log out of the system
steps: 1. Click on the 'Log out' button in the user menu
2. Wait for the page to redirect
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page; the session is terminated and the user is no longer authenticated

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access a protected page after logging out
steps: 1. Try to navigate to a protected page (e.g., Course page)
2. Observe the response
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page; access to the protected page is denied

test_cases.test_case: Log out while performing an action
preconditions: User is in the middle of creating an assignment
steps: 1. Start creating an assignment
2. Click on the 'Log out' button in the user menu
3. Wait for the page to redirect
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page; the assignment creation process is terminated and no data is saved
